message:
fix: pause polling on hidden KeepAlive routes to prevent nav blocking (#5890)

When navigating away from a custom dashboard, KeepAlive keeps the old
route alive with display:none. Auto-refreshing cards continued polling
and triggering state updates, blocking React from rendering the new
route ("URL changes but content doesn't").

Fix: Add KeepAliveActiveContext that provides isActive=false to hidden
routes. useCache, useDataSource, and useCardFetch now skip fetches and
clear intervals when inactive. Polling resumes when the route becomes
active again.

Fixes #5856

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch: @@ -14,6 +14,7 @@ import { useLocation, useOutlet } from 'react-router-dom' import { ContentLoadingSkeleton } from './Layout' import { ChunkErrorBoundary } from '../ChunkErrorBoundary' import { PageErrorBoundary } from '../PageErrorBoundary' +import { KeepAliveActiveContext } from '../../hooks/useKeepAliveActive' const MAX_CACHED = 8 @@ -87,13 +88,15 @@ export function KeepAliveOutlet() { data-keepalive-active={active ? 'true' : 'false'} style={{ display: active ? 'contents' : 'none' }} > - - - }> - {element} - - - + + + + }> + {element} + + + + ))}

@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+/**
+ * useKeepAliveActive — returns whether the current component is on the active
+ * KeepAlive route.
+ *
+ * Components inside a KeepAliveOutlet stay mounted even when their route is
+ * hidden (`display: none`). This hook lets polling hooks (e.g. useCache) pause
+ * auto-refresh while the route is inactive, preventing hidden dashboards from
+ * continuously fetching and triggering React state updates that block the
+ * active route from rendering (#5856).
+ *
+ * Returns `true` when:
+ *   - The component is on the currently-active KeepAlive route, OR
+ *   - The component is NOT inside a KeepAlive wrapper (safe default).
+ */
+
+import { useContext, createContext } from 'react'
+
+export const KeepAliveActiveContext = createContext<boolean>(true)
+
+export function useKeepAliveActive(): boolean {
+  return useContext(KeepAliveActiveContext)
+}
